steps.title: Spot the contrasting claims about full-time work
steps.content:
Lorde states that poets can write “between shifts, in the hospital pantry, on the subway”—practicing poetry does not require the uninterrupted time other arts demand.

Text 2 argues that publishing requires “extensive practice,” which requires time “not often available to those who must also work full-time jobs.”

These claims directly oppose each other: Lorde's position assumes full-time work doesn't block poetry, while Text 2's assumes it does.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: To question D, Lorde answers no—poetry is uniquely flexible—while Text 2 answers yes—full-time jobs prevent extensive practice. Choices A, B, and C don't create opposing answers. That matches choice D — D is correct.
